enrichment.short_description: Simple Analytics is a privacy-first, cookieless web analytics platform from Amsterdam that gives site owners clear traffic insights without tracking, profiling, or storing personal data about visitors.
enrichment.starting_price: Free plan available; paid plans from EUR 20 per month
enrichment.seo_title: Simple Analytics Review 2026: Pricing, Features and Privacy-First Analytics
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See how Simple Analytics delivers cookieless, GDPR-compliant web analytics. Compare pricing plans, features, pros and cons versus Google Analytics.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Simple Analytics is a privacy-focused, cookieless web analytics tool founded in 2018 by Dutch developer Adriaan van Rossum and headquartered in Amsterdam, Netherlands. The company was built as a direct response to concerns about Google Analytics' data collection practices and operates with a very small, bootstrapped team of around two people. Simple Analytics does not use cookies, does not track individual visitors across sessions, and does not store personal data, which lets customers run analytics without cookie consent banners. It is designed to comply with GDPR, PECR, and CCPA by default because it never collects personally identifiable information. The dashboard is a single page showing pageviews, visitors, referrers, top pages, screen sizes, and countries, aimed at people who want a fast, no-configuration alternative to Google Analytics. Simple Analytics offers a free forever plan with a small website limit and 30-day data history, alongside paid Self-Serve plans starting around EUR 20 per month that scale with pageview volume and unlock longer data retention, more users, and no dashboard badge requirement. An Enterprise tier adds raw data export, SSO, role-based access, Looker Studio integration, and support for security and procurement reviews including SOC 2 documentation. It is EU-hosted, making it a common choice for privacy-conscious founders, agencies, and SaaS companies operating under European data protection law. Competitors include Plausible Analytics, Fathom Analytics, and Matomo.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Simple Analytics
paragraphs: Simple Analytics is a web analytics platform built around a single premise: you should be able to understand your website traffic without tracking the people who visit it. Founded in 2018 in the Netherlands, the product was created after founder Adriaan van Rossum grew frustrated with the complexity, invasiveness, and consent-banner overhead of Google Analytics.

Instead of cookies, fingerprinting, or persistent identifiers, Simple Analytics aggregates anonymous, non-identifiable traffic data in real time. Because no personal data is collected, most sites running it do not need a cookie consent banner under GDPR or PECR, which is a major reason privacy-conscious founders and EU-based businesses adopt it.

The dashboard intentionally favors clarity over depth: pageviews, unique visitors, top referrers, top pages, device types, and countries are all visible on one screen with no funnel-building or event schema required to get started, though custom events and goals are supported for teams that need them.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Simple Analytics tracks pageviews and visitors without cookies or local storage, using a script that avoids anything that would classify as personal data collection under GDPR. This also means the tool is unaffected by browser cookie-blocking and ad-blocker behavior in many cases, since it does not rely on third-party tracking infrastructure.

Beyond basic traffic counts, the platform supports custom events, goal tracking, UTM campaign reporting, and a public dashboard option so teams can share live stats with stakeholders without giving out login access. An API and data export are available on paid and Enterprise plans for teams that want to pipe data into a warehouse or BI tool.

Simple Analytics also publishes its infrastructure and compliance posture openly, including EU-only hosting, and offers a proxy setup so analytics traffic is served from a business's own domain rather than a third-party one, improving both privacy optics and ad-blocker resilience.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Simple Analytics offers a genuinely free forever plan covering a handful of websites with about 30 days of history, aimed at hobby projects and evaluation.

The paid Self-Serve plan starts at roughly EUR 20 per month and scales with monthly pageview volume; pricing increases as traffic grows, and annual billing includes a discount equivalent to two free months.

An Enterprise plan with custom pricing adds raw data export, SSO, role-based access control, Looker Studio integration, priority support, and help with security or procurement reviews, targeting larger organizations with compliance requirements.

features.title: Cookieless tracking
features.description: Collects aggregate traffic data without cookies, device fingerprinting, or persistent identifiers, avoiding the need for a cookie consent banner on most sites.

features.title: One-page dashboard
features.description: Shows pageviews, visitors, referrers, top pages, device types, and countries on a single, no-configuration screen.

features.title: GDPR, PECR, and CCPA aligned
features.description: Built to comply with major privacy regulations by design, since no personal data is ever stored about individual visitors.

features.title: Custom events and goals
features.description: Tracks conversions and custom interactions such as signups or purchases without relying on invasive event pipelines.

features.title: Public and shared dashboards
features.description: Lets teams share live analytics with clients or stakeholders via a public link instead of granting account access.

features.title: EU-hosted infrastructure
features.description: Data is processed and stored on EU servers, a common requirement for European businesses handling site analytics.

features.title: API and data export
features.description: Paid plans include API access and raw data export for teams that want to integrate traffic data into other tools.

features.title: Proxy and ad-blocker resilience
features.description: Supports first-party proxy setups so the analytics script is served from a business's own domain, reducing ad-blocker interference.

pros: No cookie consent banner required on most implementations because no personal data is collected
Extremely fast, uncluttered dashboard that is easy for non-technical stakeholders to read
Transparent EU-based hosting and compliance posture aimed squarely at GDPR requirements
Free forever plan makes it easy to evaluate before committing to a paid tier
Small, founder-led team known for responsive support and product transparency
cons: Less granular than Google Analytics or Mixpanel for deep behavioral or funnel analysis
Free plan is limited to a small number of websites and short data retention
Pricing rises with traffic volume, which can add up for high-traffic sites
No native mobile app analytics SDK comparable to larger platforms
Smaller ecosystem of integrations and community resources than Google Analytics

faqs.question: Does Simple Analytics use cookies
faqs.answer: No. Simple Analytics does not use cookies or any persistent identifiers, which is why most websites using it do not need to show a cookie consent banner under GDPR or PECR.

faqs.question: Is Simple Analytics GDPR compliant
faqs.answer: Yes. Simple Analytics is built to comply with GDPR, PECR, and CCPA by not collecting personal data or tracking individuals across sessions, and by hosting data within the EU.

faqs.question: Does Simple Analytics have a free plan
faqs.answer: Yes, Simple Analytics offers a free forever plan that covers a small number of websites with about 30 days of history, alongside paid Self-Serve and Enterprise plans.

faqs.question: How much does Simple Analytics cost
faqs.answer: Paid Self-Serve plans start at roughly EUR 20 per month and scale with monthly pageview volume. Enterprise pricing is custom and available on request.

faqs.question: Who founded Simple Analytics
faqs.answer: Simple Analytics was founded in 2018 by Dutch developer Adriaan van Rossum and is headquartered in Amsterdam, Netherlands.

faqs.question: How is Simple Analytics different from Google Analytics
faqs.answer: Simple Analytics avoids cookies and personal data collection entirely, offers a simpler single-page dashboard, and does not require a consent banner, whereas Google Analytics relies on cookies and detailed user-level tracking.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Simple Analytics
faqs.answer: Simple Analytics is offered as a hosted SaaS product with EU-based infrastructure; it is not distributed as self-hosted open-source software.

faqs.question: What are common alternatives to Simple Analytics
faqs.answer: Common alternatives include Plausible Analytics, Fathom Analytics, and Matomo, all of which also position themselves as privacy-friendly Google Analytics replacements.

use_cases.title: GDPR-friendly analytics for EU businesses
use_cases.description: Companies operating under European data protection law use Simple Analytics to measure traffic without needing cookie consent banners or complex compliance reviews.

use_cases.title: Lightweight analytics for indie and side projects
use_cases.description: Solo founders and small teams use the free plan to track pageviews and referrers without the overhead of setting up Google Analytics.

use_cases.title: Client and stakeholder reporting
use_cases.description: Agencies use public dashboards to share live traffic performance with clients without handing out login credentials.

pricing_plans.billing_cycle: monthly or annual (2 months free annually)
features: No cookies or consent banner required
Support for security and procurement reviews including SOC 2

enrichment.short_description: Simple Machines Forum (SMF) is a free, open-source PHP and MySQL bulletin-board application used to run online discussion communities and forums.
enrichment.headquarters: Arizona, United States (Simple Machines LLC registration)
enrichment.seo_title: Simple Machines Forum (SMF) Review 2026: Free Open-Source Forum Software
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Simple Machines Forum is a free, open-source PHP forum platform. See its history, features, pros, cons, and how it compares to other bulletin-board software.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Simple Machines Forum, commonly known as SMF, is a free and open-source Internet forum and bulletin-board application written in PHP and backed by a MySQL database. It traces its lineage to YaBB (Yet Another Bulletin Board), an early open-source Perl forum script released in 2001, and later YaBB SE. A group of developers including Jeff Lewis and Matt Webb split off to rewrite the codebase from scratch, officially launching under the Simple Machines Forum name and brand in 2003. In 2006 the project was formally separated from its original commercial parent, Lewis Media, and transferred to a new entity, Simple Machines LLC, registered in Arizona, to reinforce its commitment to remaining free software without corporate control. SMF is distributed under its own license that keeps the core software free to download, install, and modify, while a large ecosystem of community-built themes and modifications extends its functionality. It is designed to be lightweight and easy to install even for site owners without deep programming experience, and it has historically been valued for strong security practices and moderation tooling. SMF competes with other self-hosted forum platforms such as phpBB, vBulletin, and Discourse, and remains popular among hobbyist communities, fan sites, and niche interest forums that want full control over their own hosting rather than relying on a hosted SaaS community platform.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Simple Machines Forum
paragraphs: Simple Machines Forum (SMF) is open-source forum software that site owners install on their own server to run a discussion board or online community. It is written in PHP and uses MySQL for data storage, and it has been actively developed and maintained since 2003.

SMF's roots go back to YaBB, one of the earliest open-source Perl-based bulletin boards, released in 2001. A group of contributors later rewrote the software from the ground up in PHP and rebranded it as Simple Machines Forum, aiming for a codebase that was easier to run and extend than the tools available at the time.

In 2006 the project split from its original commercial sponsor and moved under Simple Machines LLC, a company registered in Arizona, to keep the software free and independent of outside corporate control. SMF remains free to download and self-host today.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: SMF provides the core building blocks expected of forum software: threaded discussion boards, private messaging, user profiles, a permissions and moderation system, and search across posts and topics.

The platform supports a large library of community-created themes and modifications (often called mods) that can add features like social login, gamification, calendars, and custom profile fields without altering the core codebase.

Because SMF is self-hosted, administrators have full control over data, moderation policy, and server-level customization, which appeals to communities that do not want to depend on a third-party SaaS forum host.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: SMF itself is free and open source; there is no license fee to download, install, or run the software.
The real cost of running SMF comes from web hosting, a MySQL-compatible database, and any premium third-party themes or modifications a community chooses to install.
Because there is no vendor subscription, ongoing costs depend entirely on hosting choices, making SMF attractive to budget-conscious community operators willing to manage their own server.

features.title: Threaded discussion boards
features.description: Organizes conversations into boards, topics, and threaded replies with support for sub-boards and categories.

features.title: Private messaging
features.description: Built-in member-to-member messaging system separate from public board posts.

features.title: Granular permissions and moderation
features.description: Role-based permission groups let administrators control who can post, moderate, or manage specific boards.

features.title: Theme and modification ecosystem
features.description: A large community library of themes and mods extends functionality without editing core files.

features.title: Self-hosted deployment
features.description: Runs on a site owner's own PHP and MySQL hosting, giving full control over data and customization.

features.title: Built-in search
features.description: Full-text search across posts, topics, and members to help users find existing discussions.

features.title: Multi-language support
features.description: Community-maintained language packs allow forums to be run in languages beyond English.

pros: Completely free and open source with no licensing cost
Long track record and mature codebase dating back to 2003
Large community of themes and modifications for customization
Full data ownership since the forum runs on the operator's own hosting
Lightweight installation that does not require advanced server expertise
cons: Requires self-hosting, so the operator is responsible for security patches and uptime
Interface feels dated compared to modern hosted community platforms like Discourse
Smaller active development pace than some newer forum software
No built-in customer support since it is a community-driven open-source project
Mobile-first responsiveness depends on the chosen theme rather than being standard

faqs.question: Is Simple Machines Forum free
faqs.answer: Yes. SMF is free, open-source forum software with no license fee. Costs come only from web hosting and any optional premium themes or modifications.

faqs.question: What is SMF built with
faqs.answer: SMF is written in PHP and uses a MySQL-compatible database, and it is designed to run on standard shared or dedicated web hosting.

faqs.question: When was Simple Machines Forum founded
faqs.answer: SMF launched under its current name and codebase in 2003, evolving from the earlier YaBB and YaBB SE open-source bulletin-board projects.

faqs.question: Who maintains Simple Machines Forum
faqs.answer: SMF is maintained by Simple Machines LLC, an entity registered in Arizona, along with a community of open-source contributors.

faqs.question: How does SMF compare to phpBB
faqs.answer: Both are free, self-hosted, PHP-based forum platforms with long histories; the choice often comes down to preferred theme ecosystems, plugin availability, and personal familiarity with each admin interface.

faqs.question: Can I customize SMF with plugins
faqs.answer: Yes, SMF supports a wide range of community-built themes and modifications that add features without modifying the core software.

faqs.question: Does SMF support multiple languages
faqs.answer: Yes, community-maintained language packs let forum administrators run SMF in languages other than English.

faqs.question: Is SMF a good choice for a new online community in 2026
faqs.answer: SMF remains a solid option for operators who want full control over self-hosted data and are comfortable managing their own server, though newer hosted platforms may offer a more modern out-of-the-box experience.

use_cases.title: Hobbyist and fan community forums
use_cases.description: Enthusiast communities use SMF to run dedicated discussion boards around shared interests without paying ongoing SaaS subscription fees.

use_cases.title: Support and knowledge-sharing forums
use_cases.description: Businesses and open-source projects self-host SMF as a searchable archive for user support questions and answers.

use_cases.title: Regional or niche interest communities
use_cases.description: Operators who need full data ownership and custom moderation policy choose SMF over hosted community platforms.

enrichment.short_description: Simplecast is a podcast hosting, distribution, and analytics platform now owned by SiriusXM's AdsWizz, built for creators and publishers who need reliable distribution and detailed listener data.
enrichment.headquarters: New York, New York, United States
enrichment.seo_title: Simplecast Review 2026: Podcast Hosting Pricing and Features
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Compare Simplecast podcast hosting plans, analytics, and distribution features. See pricing, pros, cons, and FAQs before choosing a hosting platform.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Simplecast is a podcast hosting, distribution, and analytics platform founded in 2015 by Brad Smith, who grew it out of a product studio called Wayward Wild before becoming Simplecast's founder and CEO. The company is headquartered in New York and was acquired by SiriusXM in June 2020, where it now operates as the flagship podcast creator platform under AdsWizz, SiriusXM's ad-technology subsidiary. Simplecast lets podcasters upload episodes, automatically distribute them to Apple Podcasts, Spotify, and other major listening apps via RSS, and track detailed audience analytics including downloads, unique listeners, geographic location, device and app breakdowns, and on-site web player engagement through its Recast embeddable player. Pricing is tiered: a Basic plan starts at $15 per month (or $13.50 per month billed annually) with 20,000 downloads included, an Essential plan at $35 per month unlocks advanced location and technology analytics plus episode comparison reporting, and Professional and Enterprise tiers offer custom pricing with dynamic ad insertion through the AdsWizz Podcast Marketplace, white-labeled RSS feeds, and dedicated account management. All plans include a 14-day free trial. Simplecast is positioned for serious independent podcasters, media companies, and publishers who want professional-grade analytics and monetization tools rather than a bare-bones free hosting option, competing with platforms such as Libsyn, Buzzsprout, Transistor, and Acast.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Simplecast
paragraphs: Simplecast is a podcast hosting and distribution platform that lets creators upload audio, publish an RSS feed, and get their show listed automatically across Apple Podcasts, Spotify, and other major podcast apps.

Founded in 2015 by Brad Smith and based in New York, Simplecast built its reputation on clean publishing tools paired with detailed audience analytics, which set it apart from more bare-bones hosting providers.

In 2020 Simplecast was acquired by SiriusXM and folded into AdsWizz, SiriusXM's advertising technology arm, becoming the company's primary podcast creation and hosting platform while continuing to operate under the Simplecast brand.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Simplecast automates distribution to all major podcast directories through a single RSS feed, and it provides a customizable show website with a custom domain option so podcasters do not need a separate site builder.

Analytics range from basic download counts on the entry-level plan to advanced reporting on higher tiers, including unique listener estimates, geographic breakdowns by country and region, device and app technology data, and side-by-side comparisons across episodes.

For publishers and larger shows, Simplecast supports dynamic ad insertion and campaign management through the AdsWizz Podcast Marketplace, secondary ad-free feeds, third-party attribution partner integrations, and API access for custom workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: The Basic plan costs $15 per month, or $13.50 per month billed annually, and includes two team seats, unlimited storage and uploads, a customizable show website, and 20,000 downloads included per month.

The Essential plan costs $35 per month ($31.50 annually) and adds four team seats along with location analytics, unique listener reporting, web player analytics, and technology analytics, with 50,000 downloads included per month.

Professional and Enterprise plans use custom, quote-based pricing and add monetization through dynamic ad insertion, white-labeled RSS feeds, high-bitrate audio, API integration, and dedicated account support. Every plan includes a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

features.title: Automated multi-platform distribution
features.description: Publishes episodes to Apple Podcasts, Spotify, and other directories automatically through a single managed RSS feed.

features.title: Customizable show website
features.description: Provides a hosted, customizable podcast website with support for a custom domain.

features.title: Detailed listener analytics
features.description: Tracks downloads, unique listeners, geography, device and app technology, and episode-to-episode comparisons.

features.title: Recast embeddable web player
features.description: Lets podcasters embed shareable audio players with episode clips directly on websites and social posts.

features.title: Dynamic ad insertion
features.description: Professional and Enterprise plans support monetization through the AdsWizz Podcast Marketplace with dynamic ad insertion.

features.title: Team collaboration seats
features.description: Plans include multiple team member seats for producers, editors, and hosts to collaborate on show management.

features.title: API and white-label options
features.description: Enterprise customers get API integration and white-labeled RSS feeds for custom publishing workflows.

features.title: Secure HTTPS hosting
features.description: All shows are served over secure HTTPS with a protected RSS feed by default.

pros: Strong, detailed analytics compared to many entry-level podcast hosts
Backing from SiriusXM and AdsWizz brings monetization opportunities for larger shows
Clean, easy-to-use publishing interface and customizable show website
14-day free trial available on all plans with no credit card required
Recast web player makes it easy to share episode clips for marketing
cons: No permanent free plan, unlike some competitors aimed at hobbyist podcasters
Advanced analytics such as location and technology data are locked behind the Essential tier and above
Professional and Enterprise pricing is not published and requires contacting sales
Download-based pricing means costs can rise as a show's audience grows
Fewer built-in editing tools compared to platforms that bundle recording and editing

faqs.question: How much does Simplecast cost
faqs.answer: Simplecast's Basic plan starts at $15 per month ($13.50 billed annually), with an Essential plan at $35 per month, and custom-priced Professional and Enterprise tiers for larger publishers.

faqs.question: Does Simplecast offer a free trial
faqs.answer: Yes, all Simplecast plans include a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Who owns Simplecast
faqs.answer: Simplecast was acquired by SiriusXM in 2020 and now operates as AdsWizz's flagship podcast creator platform.

faqs.question: Does Simplecast support podcast monetization
faqs.answer: Yes, Professional and Enterprise plans support dynamic ad insertion and campaign management through the AdsWizz Podcast Marketplace.

faqs.question: What analytics does Simplecast provide
faqs.answer: Simplecast tracks downloads, unique listeners, geographic location, device and app technology data, and web player engagement, with more advanced reporting on higher-tier plans.

faqs.question: Can I use a custom domain with Simplecast
faqs.answer: Yes, Simplecast's customizable show website supports a custom domain on all plans.

faqs.question: Who founded Simplecast
faqs.answer: Simplecast was founded in 2015 by Brad Smith, who later became Head of Podcast Products at SiriusXM.

faqs.question: Is Simplecast good for beginners
faqs.answer: Simplecast is usable by beginners but is generally positioned toward serious podcasters and publishers who want professional analytics rather than the cheapest possible entry-level hosting.

use_cases.title: Independent podcasters scaling an audience
use_cases.description: Creators use Simplecast's analytics to understand listener geography and platform usage as their show grows beyond a hobby project.

use_cases.title: Media companies and publishers
use_cases.description: Publishers with multiple shows use Simplecast's Professional and Enterprise tools for monetization, white-labeling, and dedicated support.

use_cases.title: Branded and marketing podcasts
use_cases.description: Companies running a branded podcast use the customizable show website and Recast player to promote episodes on their own marketing channels.

features: Customizable show website with custom domain
Location analytics by country and region
Dynamic ad insertion and campaign management

enrichment.short_description: SimpleDMS is an open-source, tag-based document management system built for small and mid-size businesses, offering cloud, on-premises, and white-label deployment options.
enrichment.starting_price: From CHF 5 per user per month (Pro plan)
enrichment.seo_title: SimpleDMS Review 2026: Open-Source Document Management Pricing and Features
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See what SimpleDMS offers small businesses: tag-based document organization, workspaces, versioning, and pricing plans for cloud, on-premises, and white-label use.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SimpleDMS is an open-source document management system positioned for small and mid-size businesses that want their files organized automatically instead of managing rigid folder trees. Rather than relying on manual filing, SimpleDMS uses a metadata- and tag-based system that suggests categorization as documents are uploaded, alongside workspaces that separate shared team documents from private files. Core capabilities include duplicate detection, document versioning, full-text search, and multiple document views, and the software can be installed as a desktop and mobile app across Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, and iOS, behaving like a native application once installed. SimpleDMS is available in three deployment models: a cloud-hosted SaaS offering, an on-premises install using source code released under a non-copyleft license, and a white-label SaaS option for partners who want to run their own multi-tenant instance. It integrates with tools such as Nextcloud, allowing files to be sent directly from Nextcloud Files into SimpleDMS. Pricing includes a Pro plan starting at roughly CHF 5 per user per month with 5 GB of cloud storage and unlimited workspaces, and an Unlimited plan at a flat rate of about CHF 395 per month with 500 GB of storage and unlimited users and workspaces. The product's primary market appears to be businesses in Austria, Germany, and Switzerland, though the self-hosted option is available worldwide. A free 30-day trial is offered. Independently verified founding year, headquarters, and company size were not available at the time of research.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is SimpleDMS
paragraphs: SimpleDMS is a document management system aimed at small businesses that want an alternative to manually organized folder structures. Its core idea is that documents should sort themselves, using tags and metadata rather than requiring users to maintain a strict filing hierarchy.

The software is open source and offered through three deployment models: a hosted cloud SaaS product, an on-premises installation using source-available code under a non-copyleft license, and a white-label SaaS option for partners who want to operate their own branded, multi-tenant instance.

SimpleDMS can be installed as a desktop and mobile application on Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, and iOS, where it behaves like a native app after installation and supports operating-system-level integrations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: At the center of SimpleDMS is a tag-based categorization system paired with metadata suggestions, which helps automate the sorting of incoming documents rather than requiring manual filing into folders.
Workspaces let teams separate shared, collaborative document areas from private individual storage, while duplicate detection and version history help avoid redundant or outdated files.

Full-text search across stored documents and support for multiple document views make it easier to locate files quickly, and integrations such as the Nextcloud connector let teams send files into SimpleDMS directly from existing storage tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: SimpleDMS offers a Pro plan priced at roughly CHF 5 per user per month, which includes 5 GB of cloud storage and unlimited workspaces, aimed at freelancers and small teams.
An Unlimited plan is available at a flat rate of about CHF 395 per month, providing 500 GB of storage with unlimited users and workspaces for larger or growing organizations.
A free 30-day trial is available before committing to a paid plan, and businesses that prefer not to use the hosted cloud option can self-host using the on-premises source code license.

features.title: Tag-based document organization
features.description: Automatically suggests tags and metadata to categorize documents instead of relying on manual folder structures.

features.title: Shared and private workspaces
features.description: Separates collaborative team document areas from private individual storage within the same system.

features.title: Duplicate detection
features.description: Identifies duplicate uploads to help keep the document library clean and avoid redundant storage.

features.title: Document versioning
features.description: Tracks version history so teams can review or revert to earlier versions of a document.

features.title: Full-text search
features.description: Searches inside document content, not just file names, to help users find what they need quickly.

features.title: Cross-platform desktop and mobile apps
features.description: Installs as a native-feeling app on Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, and iOS.

features.title: Flexible deployment options
features.description: Available as cloud SaaS, on-premises self-hosted software, or white-label multi-tenant SaaS.

features.title: Nextcloud integration
features.description: Sends files directly from Nextcloud Files into SimpleDMS without manual re-uploading.

pros: Automated tag-based sorting reduces manual filing work for small teams
Flexible deployment as cloud, on-premises, or white-label SaaS suits different compliance needs
Open-source, non-copyleft licensing option appeals to businesses wanting more control
Cross-platform desktop and mobile apps behave like native software
Free 30-day trial lowers the barrier to testing the platform
cons: Limited independently verifiable company information such as founding year or headquarters
Smaller ecosystem and brand recognition compared to established document management vendors
Entry-level Pro plan storage (5 GB) is modest for document-heavy teams
White-label and on-premises licensing details require direct contact rather than transparent public pricing
Primary market focus on German-speaking Europe may mean less localized support elsewhere

faqs.question: What is SimpleDMS used for
faqs.answer: SimpleDMS is a document management system that helps small and mid-size businesses store, tag, search, and version their documents without relying on manual folder organization.

faqs.question: Is SimpleDMS open source
faqs.answer: Yes, SimpleDMS is offered as open-source software, with a business option to access the source code under a non-copyleft license for on-premises deployment.

faqs.question: How much does SimpleDMS cost
faqs.answer: SimpleDMS pricing starts at roughly CHF 5 per user per month for the Pro plan with 5 GB of storage, with an Unlimited plan at about CHF 395 per month flat for 500 GB and unlimited users.

faqs.question: Can SimpleDMS be self-hosted
faqs.answer: Yes, SimpleDMS offers an on-premises deployment option using source code released under a non-copyleft license, in addition to its cloud SaaS offering.

faqs.question: Does SimpleDMS integrate with Nextcloud
faqs.answer: Yes, SimpleDMS has a Nextcloud integration that allows files to be sent directly from Nextcloud Files into SimpleDMS.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for SimpleDMS
faqs.answer: Yes, SimpleDMS offers a free 30-day trial before requiring a paid subscription.

faqs.question: What platforms does SimpleDMS support
faqs.answer: SimpleDMS can be installed as a desktop and mobile app on Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, and iOS.

faqs.question: Who is SimpleDMS best suited for
faqs.answer: SimpleDMS is best suited for small businesses and freelancers who want automated document organization without the overhead of a large enterprise document management platform.

use_cases.title: Automated document filing for small teams
use_cases.description: Small businesses use SimpleDMS's tag suggestions to organize incoming documents without maintaining manual folder trees.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted compliance-sensitive storage
use_cases.description: Organizations with data residency requirements deploy SimpleDMS on-premises using the non-copyleft source license.

use_cases.title: White-label document management for partners
use_cases.description: Software resellers and agencies use the white-label SaaS option to offer branded document management to their own clients.

enrichment.short_description: SimpleLogin is an open-source email alias service that lets users shield their real email address behind unlimited disposable aliases, now owned by Proton AG.
enrichment.starting_price: Free plan available; Premium from $2.99/month billed annually
enrichment.company_size: Part of Proton AG (500+ employees company-wide)
enrichment.headquarters: Paris, France (SimpleLogin origin); Proton AG headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland
enrichment.seo_title: SimpleLogin Review 2026: Email Alias Pricing, Features, and Proton Integration
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Learn how SimpleLogin's email alias service protects your inbox, how it integrates with Proton Pass, and what the free and premium plans include.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SimpleLogin is an open-source email alias and forwarding service founded in 2019 that lets users generate unlimited disposable email addresses which forward messages to a real inbox while hiding the user's actual email from websites and services. In April 2022, Proton AG, the company behind the encrypted email service ProtonMail, acquired SimpleLogin for an undisclosed sum; at the time of acquisition SimpleLogin had more than 100,000 users who had created over 2 million aliases. Since the acquisition, SimpleLogin has continued to operate as a standalone service while also being deeply integrated into Proton Pass, Proton's password manager, so Proton subscribers get alias creation built directly into their password vault. SimpleLogin's free plan includes 10 aliases, one mailbox, unlimited bandwidth, reply-from-alias functionality, browser extensions for Chrome, Firefox, and Safari, plus iOS and Android apps. The Premium plan costs $4 per month or $36 per year (previously priced lower, now aligned closer to $2.99 to $35.88 per year following integration with Proton Pass Plus) and adds unlimited aliases, unlimited custom domains, catch-all domain support, the ability to send new emails from an alias, multiple mailboxes, and PGP encryption. SimpleLogin is popular among privacy-conscious users who want to reduce spam, prevent data broker tracking, and quickly disable a compromised alias without changing their real email address, positioning it alongside alternatives like Apple Hide My Email, Firefox Relay, and DuckDuckGo Email Protection.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is SimpleLogin
paragraphs: SimpleLogin is an email alias service that generates disposable, forwarding email addresses so users never have to give their real email address to a website, newsletter, or online account. Messages sent to an alias are forwarded to the user's real inbox, and replies can be sent from the alias without revealing the underlying address.
Founded in 2019, SimpleLogin grew as an open-source alternative to proprietary alias services, offering browser extensions, mobile apps, and a web dashboard for managing aliases.

In April 2022, Proton AG acquired SimpleLogin to strengthen its privacy product suite. SimpleLogin continues to operate as its own service while also powering alias creation inside Proton Pass, Proton's password manager.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Users can create unlimited email aliases on the Premium plan (10 on the free plan), each of which forwards to a designated mailbox and can be disabled instantly if it starts receiving spam or is linked to a data breach.
SimpleLogin supports custom domains and catch-all aliasing, so users or businesses can generate on-the-fly aliases under their own domain rather than relying only on SimpleLogin's shared domains.
The service integrates with Proton Pass, giving Proton subscribers built-in alias generation alongside password management, plus PGP encryption support and browser extensions for major browsers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: The Free plan includes 10 aliases, one mailbox, unlimited bandwidth, reply-from-alias capability, and access to browser extensions and mobile apps.

The Premium plan is priced at $4 per month or $36 per year, and following integration with Proton Pass, new subscribers are billed around $2.99 per month ($35.88 per year), which also includes Proton Pass Plus features like unlimited vaults and a built-in two-factor authenticator.

Premium unlocks unlimited aliases, unlimited custom domains, catch-all domain support, multiple mailboxes, and PGP encryption, with payment accepted via card, PayPal, Apple Pay, and select cryptocurrencies.

features.title: Unlimited email aliases (Premium)
features.description: Generates disposable aliases that forward to a real inbox, hiding the user's actual email address from third parties.

features.title: Reply from alias
features.description: Lets users send replies from an alias address without exposing their real email to the recipient.

features.title: Custom domain support
features.description: Allows Premium users to create aliases under their own domain rather than only SimpleLogin's shared domains.

features.title: Catch-all aliasing
features.description: Automatically forwards any email sent to a custom domain, even for aliases not explicitly created in advance.

features.title: Browser extensions and mobile apps
features.description: Available for Chrome, Firefox, Safari, iOS, and Android for quick alias creation while browsing.

features.title: PGP encryption
features.description: Premium plan supports PGP encryption for forwarded emails to add an extra layer of message privacy.

features.title: Proton Pass integration
features.description: Built directly into Proton Pass, letting Proton subscribers generate aliases alongside password management.

features.title: Instant alias disable
features.description: Aliases can be turned off instantly if they start receiving spam or are exposed in a data breach.

pros: Open-source codebase adds transparency and trust for privacy-focused users
Backed by Proton AG, a well-established privacy and security company
Free plan is genuinely usable with 10 aliases and core forwarding features
Custom domain and catch-all support give power users flexibility beyond basic aliasing
Tight integration with Proton Pass streamlines alias creation for existing Proton users
cons: Free plan is capped at only 10 aliases, which power users can exhaust quickly
No dedicated family plan for sharing Premium across a household
Some advanced features require also subscribing to or using Proton Pass
Pricing has shifted since the Proton Pass integration, which can be confusing for legacy subscribers
Reliant on Proton's broader roadmap now that it is owned by Proton AG rather than fully independent

faqs.question: Is SimpleLogin free
faqs.answer: Yes, SimpleLogin has a free plan with 10 aliases, one mailbox, and reply-from-alias functionality. A Premium plan unlocks unlimited aliases and custom domains.

faqs.question: Who owns SimpleLogin
faqs.answer: SimpleLogin was acquired by Proton AG, the company behind ProtonMail and Proton Pass, in April 2022.

faqs.question: How much does SimpleLogin Premium cost
faqs.answer: SimpleLogin Premium costs $4 per month or $36 per year, though pricing following Proton Pass integration is closer to $2.99 per month billed annually at $35.88.

faqs.question: What is an email alias
faqs.answer: An email alias is a disposable, forwarding email address that hides a user's real inbox from websites and services while still delivering messages.

faqs.question: Does SimpleLogin work with Proton Pass
faqs.answer: Yes, SimpleLogin is integrated into Proton Pass, allowing Proton subscribers to generate email aliases directly from their password manager.

faqs.question: Is SimpleLogin open source
faqs.answer: Yes, SimpleLogin is built on an open-source codebase, which is available for review and self-hosting by technical users.

faqs.question: Can I use my own domain with SimpleLogin
faqs.answer: Yes, the Premium plan supports unlimited custom domains and catch-all aliasing under domains the user owns.

faqs.question: How many users does SimpleLogin have
faqs.answer: At the time of its acquisition by Proton in 2022, SimpleLogin had more than 100,000 users who had created over 2 million email aliases.

use_cases.title: Reducing spam and data broker exposure
use_cases.description: Privacy-conscious individuals use unique aliases for every signup so they can identify and block the source of spam or data leaks.

use_cases.title: Protecting a real email during online shopping
use_cases.description: Shoppers use disposable aliases for one-off purchases or trials to avoid marketing emails reaching their main inbox.

use_cases.title: Business use of custom domain aliasing
use_cases.description: Small teams create department- or purpose-specific aliases under a custom domain for support, sales, or vendor communication.

enrichment.short_description: SimplePractice is a cloud-based practice management and electronic health record platform built for therapists and other health and wellness professionals to manage scheduling, billing, and telehealth.
enrichment.seo_title: SimplePractice Review 2026: EHR Pricing, Features, and Plans
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Compare SimplePractice pricing plans, EHR features, and telehealth tools for therapists and health and wellness professionals before you sign up.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SimplePractice is a cloud-based practice management and electronic health record (EHR) platform built for health and wellness professionals such as therapists, counselors, psychologists, and other private-practice clinicians. It was founded in 2012 in Santa Monica, California, by Howard Spector while he was training to become a Marriage and Family Therapist, and the company remains headquartered in Santa Monica with roughly 500 to 1,000 employees. SimplePractice was acquired by EngageSmart in 2021 and continued operating as an independent product, and in January 2024 EngageSmart itself, including SimplePractice, was taken private through a roughly four billion dollar acquisition by Vista Equity Partners. The platform serves more than 225,000 practitioners and covers scheduling with online booking, HIPAA-compliant telehealth video sessions, client billing and insurance claim submission, customizable intake forms and progress notes, a client portal, and automated appointment reminders. Pricing starts at $49 per month for the Starter plan aimed at solo practitioners, scaling up through plans that add insurance billing tools, and a Plus plan for group practices priced at $99 per month base plus roughly $59 per month per additional clinician. New customers can start a paid plan within the first seven days of a free trial to receive a discount on their first three months. SimplePractice is widely used across behavioral health, and its main competitors include TherapyNotes, TheraNest, and Kareo.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is SimplePractice
paragraphs: SimplePractice is a practice management and EHR platform purpose-built for health and wellness professionals running their own practice, most commonly therapists, counselors, psychologists, and similar clinicians in private or group practice settings.

The company was founded in 2012 by Howard Spector in Santa Monica, California, where it remains headquartered. It grew from a solo project into a platform used by more than 225,000 practitioners across the United States.

SimplePractice has changed ownership over time: it was acquired by EngageSmart in 2021, and in January 2024 EngageSmart, along with SimplePractice, was taken private in a roughly four billion dollar acquisition by Vista Equity Partners, while continuing to operate under its own brand.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The platform covers the core administrative workflow of a health and wellness practice: online appointment scheduling, automated reminders, a secure client portal, and customizable intake forms and progress notes tailored for clinical documentation.

SimplePractice includes HIPAA-compliant telehealth video sessions built directly into the platform, so clinicians can see clients remotely without a separate video tool, along with e-prescribing and treatment planning features on relevant plans.
Billing tools support credit card processing, superbill generation, and electronic insurance claim submission, which is a major differentiator for solo and group practices that accept insurance.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: SimplePractice's Starter plan begins at $49 per month, targeting solo practitioners who need core scheduling, documentation, and billing features.

Higher tiers add features like advanced insurance billing and more robust reporting, with the Plus plan for group practices starting at $99 per month as a base fee, plus roughly $59 per month for each additional clinician added to the account.
New users can begin a paid subscription within the first seven days of the free trial to receive a discount, commonly around 50 percent off, for their first three months.

features.title: Online scheduling and reminders
features.description: Lets clients book appointments online and sends automated email and text reminders to reduce no-shows.

features.title: HIPAA-compliant telehealth
features.description: Built-in video sessions allow clinicians to see clients remotely without a separate video conferencing tool.

features.title: Customizable documentation
features.description: Intake forms, progress notes, and treatment plans can be tailored to a clinician's specialty and workflow.

features.title: Insurance billing and claims
features.description: Supports electronic claim submission, superbills, and payment processing for practices that accept insurance.

features.title: Secure client portal
features.description: Gives clients a place to complete paperwork, message their provider, and view upcoming appointments.

features.title: Group practice management
features.description: Plus plan supports multiple clinicians under one practice account with role-based access.

features.title: Practice analytics and reporting
features.description: Provides reporting on appointments, billing, and practice performance for owners managing a growing caseload.

pros: Purpose-built specifically for therapists and health and wellness professionals
Combines scheduling, telehealth, documentation, and billing in one platform
Large, established user base of over 225,000 practitioners
HIPAA-compliant telehealth is built in rather than requiring a separate tool
Free trial with a discount available for early sign-up on a paid plan
cons: Pricing rises meaningfully for group practices with per-clinician fees on top of the base plan
Some advanced billing and insurance features are limited to higher-tier plans
Ownership changes (EngageSmart, then Vista Equity Partners) may raise questions for long-term customers about product direction
Learning curve for clinicians unfamiliar with EHR software
Primarily built around US insurance and compliance workflows, limiting fit for practices outside the United States

faqs.question: How much does SimplePractice cost
faqs.answer: SimplePractice's Starter plan begins at $49 per month, with a Plus plan for group practices starting at $99 per month plus roughly $59 per month per additional clinician.

faqs.question: Who founded SimplePractice
faqs.answer: SimplePractice was founded in 2012 by Howard Spector in Santa Monica, California, while he was training to become a Marriage and Family Therapist.

faqs.question: Is SimplePractice HIPAA compliant
faqs.answer: Yes, SimplePractice includes HIPAA-compliant telehealth video sessions and secure documentation designed for health and wellness practices.

faqs.question: Who owns SimplePractice now
faqs.answer: SimplePractice was acquired by EngageSmart in 2021, and in January 2024 EngageSmart was taken private by Vista Equity Partners in a deal valued at roughly four billion dollars.

faqs.question: Does SimplePractice support insurance billing
faqs.answer: Yes, SimplePractice supports electronic insurance claim submission, superbill generation, and payment processing for practices that accept insurance.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for SimplePractice
faqs.answer: Yes, SimplePractice offers a free trial, and customers who start a paid plan within the first seven days can receive a discount on their first three months.

faqs.question: Who uses SimplePractice
faqs.answer: SimplePractice is used by therapists, counselors, psychologists, and other health and wellness professionals running solo or group private practices, with more than 225,000 practitioners on the platform.

faqs.question: Does SimplePractice support group practices
faqs.answer: Yes, the Plus plan is designed for group practices and supports multiple clinicians under one account with role-based access.

use_cases.title: Solo therapy practice management
use_cases.description: Individual therapists use SimplePractice's Starter plan to manage scheduling, notes, and billing without extra administrative staff.

use_cases.title: Group behavioral health practices
use_cases.description: Multi-clinician practices use the Plus plan to centralize scheduling, billing, and documentation across an entire team.

use_cases.title: Telehealth-first care delivery
use_cases.description: Clinicians offering remote sessions use SimplePractice's built-in HIPAA-compliant video instead of a separate telehealth tool.

pricing_plans.price: $99/month base plus approx $59/month per additional clinician

enrichment.short_description: Simplified is an AI-powered content creation platform combining copywriting, graphic design, video editing, and social media scheduling for marketing teams and small businesses.
enrichment.seo_title: Simplified Review 2026: AI Content and Design Platform Pricing
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See how Simplified combines AI writing, graphic design, video editing, and social scheduling in one platform. Compare pricing, features, pros, and cons.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Simplified is an AI-powered content creation platform founded in 2020 by CEO KD Deshpande, operating under the legal entity TLDR Technologies, Inc. and headquartered in San Francisco, California. The company raised early funding including a pre-seed round of roughly $2.2 million and an $8.5 million seed round led by Craft Ventures with participation from investors such as Khosla Ventures, 8-Bit Capital, and Wing Venture Capital, positioning itself as a challenger to Canva by bundling multiple creative tools into a single subscription. Simplified combines AI copywriting for blog posts, ads, and social captions with a drag-and-drop graphic design editor, a video editing and AI video generation toolset, and a social media scheduler that lets marketers plan and publish content across channels without switching between separate apps. The platform uses a credit-based pricing model: a Free Forever plan includes unlimited design projects, access to over 1,000 fonts, 500 MB of storage, and a one-time allotment of AI credits, while paid tiers include Pro at $29 per month, Business at $79 per month, and Growth at $199 per month, plus custom Enterprise pricing for larger teams. Higher tiers unlock more monthly AI credits, additional brand kits, more storage, and expanded collaboration seats. Simplified targets small businesses, freelancers, and in-house marketing teams that want to consolidate design, writing, video, and scheduling into one workflow rather than paying for Canva, a separate AI writing tool, and a social scheduler independently.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Simplified
paragraphs: Simplified is an all-in-one AI content creation platform that bundles graphic design, AI copywriting, video editing, and social media scheduling into a single subscription, aimed at marketing teams and small businesses that want to avoid juggling multiple point solutions.

The company was founded in 2020 by KD Deshpande and operates under the legal name TLDR Technologies, Inc., headquartered in San Francisco, California. It has raised early-stage venture funding including a seed round led by Craft Ventures.
Simplified is often positioned as a challenger to Canva, but it differentiates itself by weaving AI writing and video tools directly into the design workflow, rather than focusing on design alone.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The design editor supports drag-and-drop graphic creation with access to over 1,000 fonts, templates, and stock assets, aimed at users without formal design training.
AI copywriting tools generate marketing copy such as blog posts, ad text, product descriptions, and social captions, using credit-based usage that scales with the subscription tier.
Video tools include editing and AI-assisted video generation, while a built-in social media scheduler lets teams plan and publish content across multiple channels from within the same platform.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Simplified's Free Forever plan includes unlimited design projects, over 1,000 fonts, 500 MB of storage, and a one-time AI credit allotment that does not renew monthly.
The Pro plan is priced at $29 per month, the Business plan at $79 per month, and the Growth plan at $199 per month, with each tier increasing monthly AI credits, storage, and collaboration features.
A custom-priced Enterprise plan is available for larger organizations that need additional seats, brand controls, and dedicated support.

features.title: AI copywriting
features.description: Generates marketing copy for blog posts, ads, product descriptions, and social captions using AI credits.

features.title: Drag-and-drop design editor
features.description: Provides templates, over 1,000 fonts, and stock assets for creating graphics without design experience.

features.description: Supports video creation and editing tools alongside AI-assisted video generation features.

features.title: Social media scheduling
features.description: Lets marketing teams plan and publish content across multiple social channels from one dashboard.

features.title: Brand kits
features.description: Stores brand colors, fonts, and logos so design and content stay consistent across a team.

features.title: Team collaboration
features.description: Allows multiple users to collaborate on design and content projects within shared workspaces.

features.title: Credit-based AI usage
features.description: Uses a credit system for AI-generated content and assets that scales with the subscription tier.

pros: Combines design, AI writing, video, and scheduling in one subscription instead of several separate tools
Free Forever plan offers real usable value including unlimited design projects
Backed by notable investors including Craft Ventures and Khosla Ventures
AI features reduce the time needed to produce first-draft marketing content
Actively positioned and marketed as a lower-cost alternative to stacking Canva plus a writing tool plus a scheduler
cons: Credit-based AI pricing can be confusing and may run out faster than expected for heavy users
Design depth is generally considered less mature than dedicated tools like Canva or Adobe Express
Free plan's AI credits are a one-time allotment rather than a recurring monthly amount
Company size and precise headcount are not consistently reported across sources
As a newer, VC-funded company, pricing and feature sets have changed over time

faqs.question: Is Simplified free to use
faqs.answer: Yes, Simplified offers a Free Forever plan with unlimited design projects, over 1,000 fonts, and 500 MB of storage, plus a one-time AI credit allotment.

faqs.question: How much does Simplified cost
faqs.answer: Paid plans include Pro at $29 per month, Business at $79 per month, and Growth at $199 per month, with custom Enterprise pricing for larger teams.

faqs.question: Who founded Simplified
faqs.answer: Simplified was founded in 2020 by CEO KD Deshpande and operates under the legal entity TLDR Technologies, Inc., headquartered in San Francisco.

faqs.question: Is Simplified a Canva alternative
faqs.answer: Yes, Simplified is commonly positioned as an alternative to Canva, adding AI copywriting, video tools, and social scheduling alongside graphic design.

faqs.question: Does Simplified include AI copywriting
faqs.answer: Yes, Simplified includes AI copywriting tools for blog posts, ads, product descriptions, and social captions using a credit-based system.

faqs.question: Can I schedule social media posts in Simplified
faqs.answer: Yes, Simplified includes a built-in social media scheduler for planning and publishing content across multiple channels.

faqs.question: Has Simplified raised funding
faqs.answer: Yes, Simplified raised a pre-seed round of about $2.2 million and an $8.5 million seed round led by Craft Ventures, with participation from Khosla Ventures and other investors.

faqs.question: Who is Simplified designed for
faqs.answer: Simplified is designed for small businesses, freelancers, and in-house marketing teams that want to consolidate design, writing, video, and scheduling tools into one platform.

use_cases.title: Small business marketing content
use_cases.description: Small business owners use Simplified to produce social graphics, ad copy, and short videos without hiring a designer or copywriter.

use_cases.title: In-house marketing team workflows
use_cases.description: Marketing teams consolidate design, copywriting, video, and scheduling into one platform to reduce tool sprawl.

use_cases.description: Freelancers use Simplified's AI writing and design tools to deliver client content faster across multiple formats.

enrichment.short_description: SimplyBook.me is an online appointment booking and scheduling platform for service-based businesses, offering client management, payments, and marketing automation.
enrichment.starting_price: Free plan available; paid plans from $8.25/month
enrichment.seo_title: SimplyBook.me Review 2026: Booking Software Pricing and Features
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Compare SimplyBook.me appointment booking plans, features, and pricing for service businesses, from solo professionals to multi-location enterprises.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SimplyBook.me is an online appointment booking and scheduling platform designed for service-based businesses such as salons, healthcare providers, consultants, tutors, and fitness studios. The product was originally developed by Notando Ltd. in Iceland and launched as a SaaS booking system in 2009, with the SimplyBook.me brand name adopted around 2011. The company is now headquartered in Cyprus and operates offices in multiple countries; in September 2023 SimplyBook.me was acquired by team.blue, a European hosting and SaaS group. The platform lets businesses accept appointments online through a customizable booking page, manage client records and history, process payments, send automated reminders, and run marketing features like promotions and gift cards. It supports multiple languages on both the booking interface and through customer support, which covers English, Spanish, French, Portuguese, Chinese, and Ukrainian, making it a common choice for businesses with international clients. Pricing is structured across five tiers: a Free plan for solo professionals with limited bookings, Basic and Standard plans priced roughly between $8.25 and $13.90 per month for small teams, Premium at up to $59.90 per month for growing businesses with five to thirty staff providers, and a custom-priced Enterprise tier for multi-location chains, franchises, healthcare networks, and universities with specific data residency or integration requirements. SimplyBook.me competes with platforms such as Acuity Scheduling, Calendly, and Square Appointments in the online booking software market.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is SimplyBook.me
paragraphs: SimplyBook.me is an online appointment booking system that helps service-based businesses accept bookings, manage clients, and take payments through a customizable booking page and dashboard.

The product originated with Notando Ltd. in Iceland and was launched as a SaaS booking platform in 2009, later adopting the SimplyBook.me brand around 2011. The company is now headquartered in Cyprus with offices in multiple countries.
In September 2023, SimplyBook.me was acquired by team.blue, a European hosting and software group, while continuing to operate under its own brand and product.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Businesses can build a customizable, branded booking page where clients choose services, staff, and appointment times, with automated confirmation and reminder emails or texts to reduce no-shows.

The platform includes client management with booking history and notes, payment processing, and marketing tools such as promotions, coupons, gift cards, and a loyalty points system to help businesses drive repeat bookings.

SimplyBook.me offers an extensive add-on marketplace, letting businesses turn on features like memberships, classes, intake forms, or video conferencing only when needed, which keeps the base product lean while still supporting complex use cases.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: SimplyBook.me offers a Free plan for solo professionals with a limited number of bookings per month, alongside Basic and Standard plans priced roughly between $8.25 and $13.90 per month for small teams.
The Premium plan costs up to $59.90 per month and is aimed at growing service businesses with five to thirty staff providers who need more advanced features and higher booking volume.

An Enterprise plan is available with custom, per-customer pricing for multi-location chains, franchises, healthcare networks, universities, and corporate clients with specific data residency or integration needs.

features.title: Customizable booking page
features.description: Lets clients book services, choose staff, and select appointment times through a branded, embeddable booking page.

features.title: Automated reminders
features.description: Sends automatic email and text reminders to reduce no-shows and last-minute cancellations.

features.title: Client management
features.description: Tracks client booking history, notes, and contact details in a central database.

features.title: Payment processing
features.description: Accepts online payments and deposits at the time of booking through integrated payment providers.

features.title: Marketing tools
features.description: Includes promotions, coupons, gift cards, and loyalty points to encourage repeat bookings.

features.title: Add-on marketplace
features.description: Offers optional add-ons such as memberships, group classes, intake forms, and video conferencing.

features.title: Multi-language booking pages
features.description: Supports multiple languages on the booking interface and through multilingual customer support.

features.title: Multi-location and enterprise support
features.description: Enterprise plan supports franchises and multi-location businesses with centralized management.

pros: Generous free plan makes it accessible for solo professionals starting out
Wide range of add-ons lets businesses customize functionality without switching platforms
Multilingual booking pages and support suit businesses with international clients
Marketing tools like gift cards and loyalty points go beyond basic scheduling
Scales from solo professionals up to multi-location enterprise businesses
cons: Interface can feel dated compared to newer scheduling tools like Calendly
Many advanced features are gated behind paid add-ons, which can increase total cost
Enterprise pricing is not publicly listed and requires contacting sales
Learning curve exists for businesses that want to configure the full add-on ecosystem
Exact founding year is reported inconsistently across sources (2009 launch versus 2011 rebrand)

faqs.question: Is SimplyBook.me free
faqs.answer: Yes, SimplyBook.me offers a Free plan for solo professionals with a limited number of monthly bookings, along with paid plans for larger teams.

faqs.question: How much does SimplyBook.me cost
faqs.answer: Paid plans range from roughly $8.25 to $13.90 per month for small teams up to $59.90 per month for the Premium plan, with custom Enterprise pricing for larger organizations.

faqs.question: Where is SimplyBook.me based
faqs.answer: SimplyBook.me is headquartered in Cyprus and operates offices in multiple countries, with roots in a booking system originally developed in Iceland.

faqs.question: Who owns SimplyBook.me
faqs.answer: SimplyBook.me was acquired by team.blue, a European hosting and software group, in September 2023.

faqs.question: Does SimplyBook.me support multiple languages
faqs.answer: Yes, SimplyBook.me supports multiple languages on its booking pages and offers multilingual customer support covering English, Spanish, French, Portuguese, Chinese, and Ukrainian.

faqs.question: What types of businesses use SimplyBook.me
faqs.answer: SimplyBook.me is used by salons, healthcare providers, consultants, tutors, fitness studios, and other service-based businesses that take appointments.

faqs.question: Does SimplyBook.me support payments
faqs.answer: Yes, SimplyBook.me integrates with payment providers to accept online payments and deposits at the time of booking.

faqs.question: Can SimplyBook.me support multiple business locations
faqs.answer: Yes, the Enterprise plan is designed for multi-location chains, franchises, and organizations with centralized booking management needs.

use_cases.title: Solo service professional scheduling
use_cases.description: Independent consultants, tutors, or therapists use the Free or Basic plan to accept online bookings without hiring administrative staff.

use_cases.title: Salon and wellness studio management
use_cases.description: Salons and studios use staff-based booking, payment processing, and marketing add-ons to manage a growing client base.

use_cases.title: Multi-location and franchise booking
use_cases.description: Healthcare networks, universities, and franchise businesses use the Enterprise plan to centralize booking across many locations.
